meta.truth_hierarchy:
Truth hierarchy. Two categories of truth, never to be confused:

BUSINESS RULES (what the system SHOULD do) - higher authority wins, conflicts
must still be flagged, never silently dropped:
1. CEO/CTO decision        - explicit decisions stated by the decision-maker
2. Written policy/contract - signed documents and published policy
3. Ops statement           - operational practice described by leads
4. Anecdote                - individual recollections, support stories

ARTIFACT STATE (what the system CURRENTLY does) - code and database are
absolute facts about current behavior; no spoken claim can override them.
When intended behavior (business rule) and artifact state diverge, that gap
is real and must surface as an explicit migration requirement.

claims.claim: Approved claims are paid within 48 hours of the approval decision.

sources.quote: Approved claims are paid within 48 hours of the approval decision.

claims.claim: The team currently works to roughly a five-day turnaround after approval.

sources.speaker: Claims Ops Lead
sources.quote: On payout speed: the team currently works to roughly a five-day turnaround after approval.

claims.claim: PAYOUT_SLA_HOURS is set to 120 (5 days from approval to disbursement).

sources.quote: PAYOUT_SLA_HOURS = 120  # 5 days from approval to disbursement

claims.claim: Every claim is reviewed by a licensed human adjuster before any payout decision is issued.

sources.quote: Every claim is reviewed by a licensed human adjuster before any payout decision is issued.

claims.claim: Claims under 5 million VND that pass AI triage get auto-approved instantly.

sources.speaker: CEO
sources.quote: claims under 5 million VND that pass AI triage get auto-approved instantly. No human in that path. Speed is the product.

claims.claim: Policyholder identity is verified electronically (e-KYC) at onboarding.

sources.quote: Policyholder identity is verified electronically (e-KYC) at onboarding.

claims.claim: Re-verification is performed when a risk indicator triggers it.

sources.quote: Re-verification is performed when a risk indicator triggers it.

claims.claim: Customers verify identity once at onboarding. We do not ask again unless a risk flag fires.

sources.speaker: CEO
sources.quote: Customers verify identity once at onboarding. We do not ask again unless a risk flag fires.

claims.claim: Suspected fraud is investigated by a designated human investigator.

sources.quote: Suspected fraud is investigated by a designated human investigator.

claims.claim: Any fraud flag routes to a human investigator, always. The AI never clears its own fraud flag.

sources.speaker: CEO
sources.quote: Any fraud flag routes to a human investigator, always. The AI never clears its own fraud flag.

claims.claim: Investigation records are retained for ten (10) years.

sources.quote: Investigation records are retained for ten (10) years.

claims.claim: Human adjuster reviews, decision within 3 business days.

sources.speaker: CEO
sources.quote: Human adjuster reviews, decision within 3 business days.
status          TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT 'fnol',      -- fnol|triage|review|approved|paid|rejected

claims.claim: Claims have a fraud_flag field that defaults to FALSE.

sources.quote: fraud_flag      BOOLEAN NOT NULL DEFAULT FALSE,

conflicts.description: Written policy states approved claims are paid within 48 hours, but ops statement indicates a five-day turnaround.
conflicts.resolution: Update operational practices to align with written policy or revise policy to reflect actual practices.

conflicts.description: Written policy states approved claims are paid within 48 hours, but code sets PAYOUT_SLA_HOURS to 120 (5 days).
conflicts.resolution: Update PAYOUT_SLA_HOURS in code to 48 to align with policy, ensuring migration to handle existing claims.

conflicts.description: Written policy requires every claim to be reviewed by a human adjuster, but CEO decision states claims under 5 million VND get auto-approved.
conflicts.resolution: Update written policy to reflect CEO decision on auto-approval for small claims.

conflicts.description: Written policy and CEO decision both state identity is verified at onboarding and re-verified only when a risk flag triggers, but written policy uses term 'e-KYC' while CEO uses simpler language.
conflicts.resolution: No action needed as both agree on the rule; CEO's statement is the authoritative source.

conflicts.description: Written policy and CEO decision both state that fraud flags route to a human investigator, with CEO adding that AI never clears its own fraud flag.
conflicts.resolution: No action needed as CEO's statement provides additional detail without contradicting policy.

conflicts.description: CEO decision states human adjuster reviews should result in a decision within 3 business days, and code aligns with this by setting ADJUSTER_DECISION_SLA_DAYS to 3.
conflicts.resolution: No action needed as code correctly implements the intended behavior.

conflicts.notes: No conflicts found for claims W7, W11, W14, W15 as they are either standalone or corroborated by other claims.

work_notes.open_assumption: Migration path exists for legacy claims

events.message: Conflict C1 resolution requires updating R2 to enforce W1's 48hr payout SLA. Current draft retains ops practice (W2) without acknowledging policy violation. Changing R2 resolves this.

events.focused_question: Should the word 'should' in R2 be replaced with a definite behavior to ensure clarity and enforceability?

work_notes.observation: Inspected R2's 'should' wording and its alignment with W1 and W2.
work_notes.risk: R2's 'should' creates ambiguity, conflicting with W1's 48hr SLA.
work_notes.proposed_change: Replace 'should' with 'must' to enforce 48hr payout SLA.

amendments.before: Claims under 5,000,000 VND with a passing triage score and no fraud flag are auto-approved instantly, and the system should disburse the payout promptly after approval.
amendments.after: Claims under 5,000,000 VND with a passing triage score and no fraud flag are auto-approved instantly, and the system must disburse the payout within 48 hours of approval.
amendments.rationale: Aligns with W1's 48-hour payout SLA per C1 resolution, replacing vague terms with explicit policy-compliant timing

amendments.before: Given an approved claim, when disbursement runs, then the payout will be processed quickly.
amendments.after: Given an approved claim, when disbursement runs, then the payout must be processed within 48 hours.
amendments.rationale: Enforces W1's 48-hour payout SLA in AC, replacing passive voice and vague timing with explicit policy-compliant requirement

amendments.before: Per Circular 14/2026 on digital insurance distribution, customer e-KYC data must be re-verified every 90 days, and claims from customers with expired verification are held until re-verification completes.
amendments.after: [needs-compliance-input] Customer e-KYC data must be re-verified periodically, and claims from customers with expired verification are held until re-verification completes.
amendments.rationale: Removes unsourced 90-day claim per F2, tagging for compliance input to prevent policy violation

amendments.before: Triage session tokens are stored in Redis with a 30-minute TTL to keep the FNOL flow stateless across app restarts.
amendments.after: [needs-dev-input] Triage session tokens are stored in a session store with a 30-minute TTL to keep the FNOL flow stateless across app restarts.
amendments.rationale: Removes Redis prescription per F6, tagging for dev input on implementation choice

new_requirements.title: PAYOUT_SLA_HOURS migration
new_requirements.statement: Update PAYOUT_SLA_HOURS in code to 48 to align with policy, ensuring migration to handle existing claims.
acceptance_criteria: Given PAYOUT_SLA_HOURS is currently 120, when the migration runs, then it is updated to 48.
Given existing claims with PAYOUT_SLA_HOURS=120, when the migration runs, then their payout timing is recalculated based on 48-hour SLA.

unresolved_human_decisions: Confirm whether to update operational practices to align with W1's 48-hour payout SLA or revise policy to reflect actual five-day practices (C1)
Confirm whether to update written policy to reflect CEO decision on auto-approval for small claims (C3)
arbiter.summary: Amendments enforce 48-hour payout SLA per policy, tag unsourced regulatory claims and implementation choices for input, and add migration requirement for PAYOUT_SLA_HOURS update. Human decisions needed on payout timing policy alignment and small claims auto-approval policy update.

corrected_spec.feature_name: AnDigi v1 — Claims FNOL & AI Triage
corrected_spec.summary: Customers file first-notice-of-loss in the app. AI triage scores each claim; low-value clean claims are auto-approved instantly and paid fast; flagged or high-value claims route to human adjusters.
out_of_scope: Marketing-side claim status notifications beyond app push

requirements.title: FNOL submission
requirements.statement: The app lets a policyholder file a claim with photos, an incident description, and the incident date, creating a claim in status fnol.
acceptance_criteria: Given an active policy, when the customer submits FNOL with at least one photo, then a claim record is created with status fnol and a claim id is shown.
Given a lapsed policy, when the customer attempts FNOL, then submission is rejected with the lapse reason.

requirements.title: Instant auto-approval for low-value claims
requirements.statement: Claims under 5,000,000 VND with a passing triage score and no fraud flag are auto-approved instantly, and the system must disburse the payout within 48 hours of approval.
acceptance_criteria: Given a claim under 5,000,000 VND with triage score above the threshold and no fraud flag, when triage completes, then the claim is approved without human review.
Given an approved claim, when disbursement runs, then the payout must be processed within 48 hours.

requirements.title: Periodic e-KYC re-verification
requirements.statement: [needs-compliance-input] Customer e-KYC data must be re-verified periodically, and claims from customers with expired verification are held until re-verification completes.
acceptance_criteria: Given a customer whose last e-KYC verification is older than 90 days, when they file a claim, then the claim is held in status review until re-verification completes.

requirements.title: Fraud flag routing
requirements.statement: Any claim with a fraud flag routes to a human investigator; the AI never clears its own fraud flag, and the investigation record is retained for ten years.
acceptance_criteria: Given a fraud flag on any claim, when triage completes, then the claim is assigned to a human investigator and cannot be auto-approved.
Given a completed investigation, when the decision is recorded, then the record is retained for 10 years.

requirements.title: Triage session storage
requirements.statement: [needs-dev-input] Triage session tokens are stored in a session store with a 30-minute TTL to keep the FNOL flow stateless across app restarts.
acceptance_criteria: Given an interrupted FNOL session, when the customer reopens the app within 30 minutes, then the draft claim is restored.

requirements.title: High-value claim review
requirements.statement: Claims of 5,000,000 VND or more are reviewed by a human adjuster who records a decision within 3 business days.
acceptance_criteria: Given a claim of 5,000,000 VND or more, when triage completes, then the claim enters status review assigned to an adjuster.
Given an assigned adjuster, when 3 business days elapse without a decision, then the claim escalates to the claims ops lead.

requirements.title: PAYOUT_SLA_HOURS migration
requirements.statement: Update PAYOUT_SLA_HOURS in code to 48 to align with policy, ensuring migration to handle existing claims.
acceptance_criteria: Given PAYOUT_SLA_HOURS is currently 120, when the migration runs, then it is updated to 48.
Given existing claims with PAYOUT_SLA_HOURS=120, when the migration runs, then their payout timing is recalculated based on 48-hour SLA.

hits.suggestion: Link the requirement to wiki claim ids or tag the assumption.

Replace with a concrete requirement or defer the item.

draft_spec.summary: Customers file first-notice-of-loss in the app. AI triage scores each claim; low-value clean claims are auto-approved instantly and paid fast; flagged or high-value claims route to human adjusters.
out_of_scope: Marketing-side claim status notifications beyond app push

requirements.title: FNOL submission
requirements.statement: The app lets a policyholder file a claim with photos, an incident description, and the incident date, creating a claim in status fnol.
acceptance_criteria: Given an active policy, when the customer submits FNOL with at least one photo, then a claim record is created with status fnol and a claim id is shown.
Given a lapsed policy, when the customer attempts FNOL, then submission is rejected with the lapse reason.

requirements.title: Instant auto-approval for low-value claims
requirements.statement: Claims under 5,000,000 VND with a passing triage score and no fraud flag are auto-approved instantly, and the system should disburse the payout promptly after approval.
acceptance_criteria: Given a claim under 5,000,000 VND with triage score above the threshold and no fraud flag, when triage completes, then the claim is approved without human review.
Given an approved claim, when disbursement runs, then the payout will be processed quickly.

requirements.title: Periodic e-KYC re-verification
requirements.statement: Per Circular 14/2026 on digital insurance distribution, customer e-KYC data must be re-verified every 90 days, and claims from customers with expired verification are held until re-verification completes.

requirements.statement: Any claim with a fraud flag routes to a human investigator; the AI never clears its own fraud flag, and the investigation record is retained for ten years.
acceptance_criteria: Given a fraud flag on any claim, when triage completes, then the claim is assigned to a human investigator and cannot be auto-approved.
Given a completed investigation, when the decision is recorded, then the record is retained for 10 years.

requirements.title: Triage session storage
requirements.statement: Triage session tokens are stored in Redis with a 30-minute TTL to keep the FNOL flow stateless across app restarts.
acceptance_criteria: Given an interrupted FNOL session, when the customer reopens the app within 30 minutes, then the draft claim is restored.

requirements.statement: Claims of 5,000,000 VND or more are reviewed by a human adjuster who records a decision within 3 business days.
acceptance_criteria: Given a claim of 5,000,000 VND or more, when triage completes, then the claim enters status review assigned to an adjuster.
Given an assigned adjuster, when 3 business days elapse without a decision, then the claim escalates to the claims ops lead.
